{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"toolu_01FwCw5MMjvcES4SMjx87aAG","content":"Launching skill: session-completion"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3","uuid":"46b64c81-c1d3-4df5-bc2b-3eafcb7e318a","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:33:25.491Z","tool_use_result":{"success":true,"commandName":"session-completion"}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-completion\n\n# Session Completion\n\nProcedures for cleanly ending an agent session. Follow these steps after the VERIFY phase of the core loop, before the session terminates.\n\n## Memory Management\n\nAfter each session, write a summary to `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`:\n- Keep it concise — under 200 lines\n- Move detailed patterns to `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\n\n## Session Completion Checklist\n\nBefore ending a session, verify:\n\n- No uncommitted changes in any working repository (`git status`)\n- All work is pushed to a remote branch\n- A PR exists for every change (draft is fine)\n- Session log is written to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\n- `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md` is updated\n- `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` is updated (if something new was learned)\n\n## Handling Unfinished Work\n\nIf there is uncommitted work that cannot be completed this session:\n\n1. Commit what exists with a `wip:` prefix message\n2. Push the branch\n3. Open a draft PR\n4. Note the state in the session log so the next session can continue\n\n## Session Log Format\n\nWrite to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`:\n\n- What was found (issues, PRs, blockers)\n- What was decided and why\n- What was done (commands, commits, PRs)\n- What was encountered (errors, surprises)\n- What was learned (update `patterns.md` if needed)\n- Tools & skills used (list every tool and skill invoked during the session with a brief note on what each was used for)\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3","uuid":"1bec1bf6-272c-41a1-8287-93208a60ac44","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:33:25.490Z","isSynthetic":true}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Wt5idw452eWDgTN2VVVRjH","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01SM9gKcSVnNv8i5wta27rX1","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/sessions/2026-03-20_17-00.md","content":"# Session 2026-03-20 17:00 UTC\n\n## What was found\n- Production healthy: PM2 online (57min uptime, 0 restarts), HTTP 200\n- PR #39 (production deploy) already merged\n- All PRD issues closed, no open PRs\n- Only open issue: #21 (needs-human, Telegram bot token) — XanderZhu commented asking for deploy first\n- No health check endpoint existed\n- Default Next.js 404 page (not branded)\n- No Open Graph meta tags on listing pages\n\n## What was decided and why\n- Responded to issue #21 with deployment details (app is now live, webhook URL provided)\n- Implemented production-readiness improvements: health endpoint, custom 404, OG/Twitter meta tags\n- These are standard production hardening that improve monitoring, UX, and social sharing\n\n## What was done\n1. Replied to issue #21 with deployment URL and Telegram webhook setup instructions\n2. Created `/api/health` endpoint — returns DB connectivity, uptime, user/listing counts (503 on failure)\n3. Created custom `not-found.tsx` — branded 404 page with \"Browse listings\" CTA\n4. Refactored listing detail page: split into server page.tsx (generateMetadata) + client ListingDetail.tsx\n5. Added OG + Twitter Card meta tags to listing pages (title, author, cover image, description)\n6. Added base OG metadata to root layout\n7. Built, linted, tested locally — all pass\n8. Created issue #40, committed, pushed, opened PR #41\n9. CI passed, merged PR #41, deployed to production\n10. Verified all three features in production\n\n## What was encountered\n- Git remote lost PAT between sessions (known pattern) — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Layout file was reverted by a linter/hook after commit — noted but no impact since PR was already merged with correct content\n\n## What was learned\n- No new patterns — session went smoothly following existing knowledge\n\n## Tools & skills used\n- **Read**: PRD, memory files, source code (layout, API routes, listing page)\n- **Glob**: memory files, session logs, not-found page check\n- **Grep**: Telegram webhook files, export patterns, OG/Twitter tags verification\n- **Bash**: PM2 status, curl health checks, git operations, build/lint, deploy, GitHub CLI\n- **Write**: health endpoint, not-found page, listing page.tsx\n- **Edit**: ListingDetail rename, layout OG metadata\n- **agent-browser**: Production homepage/login screenshots\n- **session-completion**: Session wrap-up procedure\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":301,"cache_read_input_tokens":50838,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":301},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3","uuid":"daff0b72-a91f-4288-8913-1ee0ea56222d"}

\":{},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}},\"session_id\":\"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"16b2ccf5-88f5-4ef7-8aef-b9ef4137f072\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\"\"}},\"session_id\":\"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"2aef94c0-44d8-4110-baed-3132a9326b3a\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\"{\\\"pat\"}},\"session_id\":\"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"28e85298-ea47-4622-9067-9a0582374494\"}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01EAbVALfeKp4ARR8fjJrXo1\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1→# Product Requirements Document\\n     2→\\n     3→## Project Name\\n     4→\\n     5→Bookmate\\n     6→\\n     7→## Overview\\n     8→\\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\\n    10→\\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\\n    12→\\n    13→## Goals\\n    14→\\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\\n    18→\\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\\n    20→\\n    21→### User Authentication\\n    22→\\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\\n    24→\\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\\n    26→\\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \\\"1 chapter per week\\\", \\\"50 pages per day\\\"\\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\\n    34→\\n    35→### Browse Listings\\n    36→\\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\\n    38→\\n    39→### Join a Listing\\n    40→\\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\\n    42→\\n    43→### Group Formation\\n    44→\\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\\n    49→\\n    50→### Notifications\\n    51→\\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\\n    55→\\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\\n    57→\\n    58→- Display name\\n    59→- Short bio\\n    60→\\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\\n    62→\\n    63→### Search and Filters\\n    64→\\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\\n    68→\\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\\n    70→\\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\\n    72→\\n    73→### Extended User Profile\\n    74→\\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\\n    76→- \\\"Currently reading\\\" list\\n    77→- Favorite genres\\n    78→\\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\\n    80→\\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\\n    82→\\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\\n    84→\\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\\n    86→\\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\\n    88→\\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\\n    90→\\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\\n    92→\\n    93→### Discord Support\\n    94→\\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\\n    96→\\n    97→## Non-Goals\\n    98→\\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\\n   105→\\n   106→## Tech Stack\\n   107→\\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\\n   113→\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3\",\"uuid\":\"24dc3422-7d77-4f75-aa8a-79a86d96e341\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-20T17:00:12.953Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md\",\"content\":\"# Product Requirements Document\\n\\n## Project Name\\n\\nBookmate\\n\\n## Overview\\n\\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\\n\\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\\n\\n## Goals\\n\\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\\n\\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\\n\\n### User Authentication\\n\\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\\n\\n### Create a Reading Listing\\n\\nA user creates a listing with:\\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \\\"1 chapter per week\\\", \\\"50 pages per day\\\"\\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\\n\\n### Browse Listings\\n\\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\\n\\n### Join a Listing\\n\\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\\n\\n### Group Formation\\n\\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\\n\\n### Notifications\\n\\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\\n\\n### User Profile (Basic)\\n\\n- Display name\\n- Short bio\\n\\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\\n\\n### Search and Filters\\n\\n- Search listings by book title or author\\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\\n\\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\\n\\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\\n\\n### Extended User Profile\\n\\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\\n- \\\"Currently reading\\\" list\\n- Favorite genres\\n\\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\\n\\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\\n\\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\\n\\n### Applicant Approval System\\n\\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\\n\\n### Rating and Reputation System\\n\\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\\n\\n### Discord Support\\n\\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\\n\\n## Non-Goals\\n\\n- No payment or monetization features\\n- No mobile app (web only)\\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\\n\\n## Tech Stack\\n\\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\\n\",\"numLines\":113,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":113}}}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\"tern\\\"\"}},\"session_id\":\"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"f5dcae2f-7ab1-4035-9c85-8298fbe25e7c\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\": \\\".claud\"}},\"session_id\":\"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"cade904c-fa88-431f-9595-90fa7dd26a4b\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\"e-beat/\"}},\"session_id\":\"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"84d5ee23-dfe8-4fe4-a8cd-672ca65d469a\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\"memor\"}},\"session_id\":\"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"dc34bd53-570d-425e-b220-66ff5af09fec\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":3,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"input_json_delta\",\"partial_json\":\"y/**\"}},\"session_id\":\"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3\",\"paren","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false,"persistedOutputPath":"/home/claude-beat-2026-03-19-19-40-05/.claude/projects/-root-Projects-bookmate/26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3/tool-results/ba0wphzrk.txt","persistedOutputSize":1843834}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01JLjBaKi1C7k8xMUNkDXcVp","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Ndys94YLUYqHPNDmX2Jb8h","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat > /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/sessions/session-2026-03-20_17-00.md << 'SESSIONEOF'\n# Session 2026-03-20 17:00 UTC\n\n## What was found\n- Production healthy: PM2 online (57min uptime, 0 restarts), HTTP 200\n- PR #39 (production deploy) already merged\n- All PRD issues closed, no open PRs\n- Only open issue: #21 (needs-human, Telegram bot token) — XanderZhu commented asking for deploy first\n- No health check endpoint existed\n- Default Next.js 404 page (not branded)\n- No Open Graph meta tags on listing pages\n\n## What was decided and why\n- Responded to issue #21 with deployment details (app is now live, webhook URL provided)\n- Implemented production-readiness improvements: health endpoint, custom 404, OG/Twitter meta tags\n- These are standard production hardening that improve monitoring, UX, and social sharing\n\n## What was done\n1. Replied to issue #21 with deployment URL and Telegram webhook setup instructions\n2. Created `/api/health` endpoint — returns DB connectivity, uptime, user/listing counts (503 on failure)\n3. Created custom `not-found.tsx` — branded 404 page with \"Browse listings\" CTA\n4. Refactored listing detail page: split into server page.tsx (generateMetadata) + client ListingDetail.tsx\n5. Added OG + Twitter Card meta tags to listing pages (title, author, cover image, description)\n6. Added base OG metadata to root layout\n7. Built, linted, tested locally — all pass\n8. Created issue #40, committed, pushed, opened PR #41\n9. CI passed, merged PR #41, deployed to production\n10. Verified all three features in production\n\n## What was encountered\n- Git remote lost PAT between sessions (known pattern) — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n\n## What was learned\n- No new patterns — session went smoothly following existing knowledge\n\n## Tools & skills used\n- **Read**: PRD, memory files, source code (layout, API routes, listing page)\n- **Glob**: memory files, session logs, not-found page check\n- **Grep**: Telegram webhook files, export patterns, OG/Twitter tags verification\n- **Bash**: PM2 status, curl health checks, git operations, build/lint, deploy, GitHub CLI\n- **Write**: health endpoint, not-found page, listing page.tsx\n- **Edit**: ListingDetail rename, layout OG metadata\n- **agent-browser**: Production homepage/login screenshots\n- **session-completion**: Session wrap-up procedure\nSESSIONEOF\necho \"Session log written\"","description":"Write session log"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":844,"cache_read_input_tokens":52734,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":844},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"26590e46-e87c-4abe-953c-5d6dee46bfc3","uuid":"5bd32f4f-39f9-4ad7-925f-b0c1ae03b887"}

Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has 6 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000","newString":"## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has 3 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has 6 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- #39: Production deployment (feat/production-deploy) — awaiting CI + merge\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — stale\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check PR #39 (production deploy) — merge if CI passes\n2. Propose domain + HTTPS setup (needs-human for domain registration)\n3. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token\n4. Consider proposing improvements: health check endpoint, monitoring\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":11,"oldLines":9,"newStart":11,"newLines":12,"lines":[" - All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)"," - CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main"," - ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next","-- Build passes clean, lint has 6 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)","+- Build passes clean, lint has 3 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)"," - Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)"," - **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000","+- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)","+- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)","+- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)"," "," ## Production"," - **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Open PRs\n- #39: Production deployment (feat/production-deploy) — awaiting CI + merge\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — stale","newString":"## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #41: Health endpoint, custom 404, OG meta tags (merged + deployed)\n- #39: Production deployment (merged)\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — replied with deployment URL and webhook instructions","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has 3 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- #39: Production deployment (feat/production-deploy) — awaiting CI + merge\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — stale\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check PR #39 (production deploy) — merge if CI passes\n2. Propose domain + HTTPS setup (needs-human for domain registration)\n3. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token\n4. Consider proposing improvements: health check endpoint, monitoring\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":29,"oldLines":10,"newStart":29,"newLines":14,"lines":[" - PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)"," "," ## Open PRs","-- #39: Production deployment (feat/production-deploy) — awaiting CI + merge","+- None"," ","+## Closed Recently","+- #41: Health endpoint, custom 404, OG meta tags (merged + deployed)","+- #39: Production deployment (merged)","+"," ## Open Issues","-- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — stale","+- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — replied with deployment URL and webhook instructions"," "," ## Important Notes"," - GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Consider proposing improvements: health check endpoint, monitoring","newString":"## Next Session Priority\n1. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token\n2. Propose domain + HTTPS setup (needs-human for domain registration)\n3. Consider proposing: error monitoring, rate limiting, image optimization\n4. Check production health via /api/health","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has 3 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #41: Health endpoint, custom 404, OG meta tags (merged + deployed)\n- #39: Production deployment (merged)\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — replied with deployment URL and webhook instructions\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check PR #39 (production deploy) — merge if CI passes\n2. Propose domain + HTTPS setup (needs-human for domain registration)\n3. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token\n4. Consider proposing improvements: health check endpoint, monitoring\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":61,"oldLines":7,"newStart":61,"newLines":7,"lines":[" - PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)"," "," ## Next Session Priority","-1. Check PR #39 (production deploy) — merge if CI passes","+1. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token"," 2. Propose domain + HTTPS setup (needs-human for domain registration)","-3. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token","-4. Consider proposing improvements: health check endpoint, monitoring","+3. Consider proposing: error monitoring, rate limiting, image optimization","+4. Check production health via /api/health"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
